# CRMP Backend - Developer Guide (System-wide Copilot Context)

## Project Purpose

CRMP is a research management system for UG, PG, and General projects.  
Supports full project lifecycle: creation, submission, evaluation, approval, and reporting.

---

## Folder / Module Conventions

src/
├── auth/ # JWT authentication, login/signup, guards
├── users/ # User service, controller, user-related domain logic
├── projects/ # Project service, controllers, workflow logic
├── access-control/ # Role/permission mapping, AccessService, guards, decorators
├── documents/ # Collaborative editor endpoints
├── drizzle/ # Database schemas & migrations

**Guidelines:**

- Each module has its own:
  - `dto/` folder for all Data Transfer Objects
  - `types/` folder for type definitions (interfaces, enums)
- Controllers handle routing and light validation only
- Services handle business logic
- Guards and decorators are thin; they check **authorization only**, never workflow or domain rules

---

## DTOs & Types

- All request/response DTOs go in `module/dto/`
- Types and enums go in `module/types/`
- Example:
  projects/
  dto/
  create-project.dto.ts
  update-project.dto.ts
  types/
  project-role.enum.ts
  project-program.enum.ts

---

## Auth & Access (One Important Part)

- JWT authentication is used
- System roles: `STUDENT, SUPERVISOR, DEPARTMENT_HEAD, PI, RA, ADRPM, VPRTT`
- Project roles (resource-specific): `PI, SUPERVISOR, EVALUATOR`
- AccessService evaluates:
  - Role → permission mapping
  - Department matching (conditionally)
  - Project program eligibility
  - Project membership + project role
- Use `@RequirePermission()` decorators for OR-permission checks
- Complex AND logic or workflow validation stays in services

---

## Coding & Service Guidelines

- Controllers: lightweight, only route + validation
- Services: full business logic + domain rules
- Never put workflow state checks in guards
- Use AccessService for authorization, never mutate entities there
- Always inject services via NestJS dependency injection

---

## Database / ORM Conventions

- Postgres + Drizzle ORM
- Use `drizzle` module for schemas & migrations
- Keep table and enum definitions in `drizzle/`
- Relations handled at DB + service layer
- Hydrate entities before passing to AccessService

---

## Guards & Decorators

- `AccessGuard` + `@RequirePermission()` are used for route-level authorization
- Pass hydrated entities (project, projectMember) in context
- Never query DB in guard
- Use AccessService directly in service layer for AND-logic or complex checks

---

## New Features / Roles / Permissions

- Add new roles in `role.enum.ts` and permissions in `permission.enum.ts`
- Map roles → permissions in `role-permissions.ts`
- New DTOs/types go into module-specific `dto/` and `types/`
- Follow existing patterns; Copilot should infer folder structure and decorator usage

---

## Best Practices

- Comments matter: Copilot reads doc comments in modules to guide suggestions
- Always provide context objects when calling AccessService
- Keep system roles and project roles clearly separate
- Use OR logic for decorators; AND logic in services
- Hydrate entities before authorization checks

---
